metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the hedge-detector design choice (exec:DET-into-verifier vs 1-TURN finder) IN this plan before handing off — state which path is chosen and why, rather than delegating the decision to the executor.
findings.finding: G-7(b) hedge-detector is explicitly flagged as an 'OPEN DESIGN ISSUE' with two unresolved options, and the plan tells the executor to 'Resolve BEFORE building' without resolving it. This is a placeholder deferring a real architectural decision to the executing agent.
findings.location: What section, G-7(b); AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Decision: 1-TURN finder' (or 'exec:DET-into-verifier') line to G-7(b) with a one-sentence rationale, then remove the 'Resolve BEFORE building' instruction since it will be resolved.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the exec:DET vs 1-TURN finder decision for the hedge detector BEFORE implementation: confirm that a 1-TURN finder (which routes naturally through pass1_chunk → Pass-2 verifier → pass3 scoring, matching how E6.no_hedges is exec'd) is the chosen path, or document what new DET prose-scanning infrastructure + verifier rerouting would be required for the exec:DET path.
findings.finding: The exec:DET mechanism cannot achieve the hedge-detector goal as proposed: engine_b.scan (det_invariants.py:112) operates on code-file ASTs/patterns, not plan-prose strings; route_criteria (orchestrator.py:352) strips all exec:DET criteria before they reach pass1_chunk; and partition_findings (orchestrator.py:393-418) stamps DET results with validity=1.0 unconditionally, bypassing the Pass-2 grading pipeline entirely. A prose-scanning hedge signal therefore cannot be exec:DET without new infrastructure (a prose-scanning DET path + a novel verifier rerouting). The plan acknowledges this as an 'OPEN DESIGN ISSUE' but defers resolution, leaving no mechanism actually selected.
findings.location: What section — G-7(b) exec:DET hedge detector; also AC 3
findings.suggested_fix: Adopt the 1-TURN finder path: add the hedge-detection criterion as exec:1-TURN (or exec:2-STEP to match E6) in criteria_routing.json, implement its prompt in the prompts library, and let it flow through the existing pass1_chunk → verifier → pass3 pipeline. This requires zero new routing infrastructure, gets proper validity grading, and aligns with how E6.no_hedges already works.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the exec:DET vs 1-TURN architecture decision for the hedge detector BEFORE implementation, and confirm the chosen path's feasibility: if exec:DET, add a prose-string scan mode to engine_b (spike required); if 1-TURN, confirm the new criterion routes through pass1_chunk and verify it does NOT use exec=DET in criteria_routing.json.
findings.finding: The plan asserts an exec:DET hedge detector as the chosen signal mechanism in the ticket title and 'What' section, but the existing DET infrastructure (engine_b.scan) runs filesystem pattern-matching via opengrep/ast-grep — it has no code path to accept a prose string as scan input. The two resolution options (prose-scanning DET path vs 1-TURN finder) have different integration complexity and are both unbuilt. The plan flags this correctly as an 'OPEN DESIGN ISSUE' but leaves it unresolved, meaning the ticket's own stated deliverable is unverifiable until the architecture decision is made.
findings.location: What section — G-7(b); AC line 3 ('Hedge-detector mechanism DECIDED and documented')
findings.suggested_fix: Given that engine_b.scan() is a filesystem scanner with no prose-string input mode, the 1-TURN finder path is the lower-risk, architecturally consistent choice — it reuses the existing pass1_chunk/AGENT path that E4 already uses (exec: AGENT in criteria_routing.json). Document this decision in the ticket and add a routing entry with exec: '1-TURN' (or AGENT) rather than exec: DET.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json` to `file_impact` and enumerate what routing changes (new DET entry, E4 checklist update) are required.
findings.finding: `criteria_routing.json` (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json`) is an unenumerated edit target: adding a new exec:DET hedge-detector criterion (G-7(b)) or modifying E4's routing entry (G-7(a) lexicon, G-4 scope-exclusion sub-check) requires changes to this file, but it does not appear in the plan's file_impact or What section.
findings.location: What section (G-7(b), G-4); file_impact (absent)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale statement for the chosen hedge-detector mechanism (e.g. '1-TURN finder chosen because it reuses the existing pass1_chunk → verifier → pass3 pipeline, has a direct codebase analogue in E6.no_hedges, and requires no new DET prose-scanning infrastructure') in the ticket's design note or What section.
findings.finding: The plan states no positive rationale for the chosen approach to either resolution option for the hedge detector. The 'Why' section describes the product goal (turn open-ended assumption checking into an enumerable probe-each scan) but does not explain why exec:DET-into-verifier was considered — or what property makes it preferable to a 1-TURN finder given that the 1-TURN path has direct codebase alignment (E6.no_hedges, which flags AC-level hedges, is already exec:2-STEP and routes through the same pipeline). Without a positive rationale, the implementer has no basis for deciding between the two options.
findings.location: Why section; What section — G-7(b) OPEN DESIGN ISSUE

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the confident-assertion trigger lexicon (the concrete verb/frame list) directly in the plan or in plan_review_E4.md so AC-1's recall test has a fixed vocabulary to verify against.
findings.finding: The 'confident-assertion trigger lexicon' — the core enumerable verb/frame list that plan_review_E4.md's SCAN PROTOCOL must contain — is described as 'MUST be enumerated in the prompt' but is nowhere specified in the plan. The current plan_review_E4.md contains only 'hedges/confident-assertions' with zero enumerated items. An implementer has no seed vocabulary to write into the file.
findings.location: What section G-7(a); AC 1; src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_E4.md
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section or plan_review_E4.md a named list of at minimum 6–8 assertion-shaped frames, e.g.: 'already exists', 'relies on', 'is implemented', 'will use', 'does X', 'has been', plus hedge frames 'should', 'likely', 'presumably'. Anchor the AC-1 recall case to at least one item from this list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the codebase-vs-external-fact decision boundary in the plan (and eventually in plan_review_E4.md): specify at minimum what makes a scope-exclusion claim 'empirically-checkable' (e.g., names a file, module, function, or interface that can be grepped/read) vs 'external-fact' (e.g., claims about third-party services, user behaviour, or organisational policy).
findings.finding: The scope-exclusion sub-check discrimination rule ('fire on empirically-checkable (codebase) exclusions; abstain-with-coverage for external-fact exclusions') is stated in the What section but provides no definition of the decision boundary. The target schema (plan_review_E4.md) has no co-located rule text that tells the LLM agent how to classify a claim as 'codebase' vs 'external-fact'.
findings.location: What section G-4; AC 2; src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_E4.md

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact interface WS1 will expose for `committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim` (key name, schema location, merge semantics) in either this ticket or by reading the WS1 plan, and add an AC proving the WS2 writers conform to that contract (e.g. a shared fixture or import test).
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim is INTRODUCED BY WS1 (sibling), not pre-existing' — a search of the entire src/ tree returns zero matches for this symbol. It does not exist in the codebase, and the sibling WS1 story's content cannot be verified from this plan alone. WS2's E4 and hedge-detector deliverables both feed this sub-answer as their primary output target, so if WS1's interface differs from what WS2 expects, both consumers break silently.
findings.location: What section / AC (all ACs implicitly depend on this symbol) / Provenance note 'committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim is INTRODUCED BY WS1'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a cross-story interface contract note (e.g. in file_impact or a shared schema file) that pins the sub-answer key name and type WS1 will produce, then add an import/schema assertion to the recall test so WS2's writers are verified against that contract at test time.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the concrete file path(s) where WS1 will introduce `committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim` to this ticket's `file_impact` or the WS1 dependency annotation, so WS2's integration callsite is enumerable.
findings.finding: `committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim` does not exist in the codebase (`src/` search returns zero hits); the plan treats it as a pre-existing integration point to 'feed' but it is introduced by the unbuilt sibling WS1. The plan names no concrete file(s) where WS1 will introduce this symbol, so WS2 has no verifiable callsite to target.
findings.location: Why section; What section ('feeding WS1's committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim'); AC5

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Co-locate the interface contract for `committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim` in this plan: specify its type (bool/enum/float), schema location (which file/schema), and the write protocol for multiple writers (E4 confident-assertion, E4 scope-exclusion, hedge detector).
findings.finding: The plan references `committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim` as the shared sub-answer that WS2 feeds into, but no interface contract for this symbol is co-located in this plan: no field name, type, write protocol, or schema location is specified for the WS2 side. A search of the entire src/ tree confirms zero existing matches. The only specification is 'depends_on WS1 recorded' (AC 5), which says nothing about the shape of the interface WS2 must implement against.
findings.location: What section (final bullet); AC 5; entire plan

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command to AC4, e.g., 'pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py -k dedup_hedge_no_hedges', and name the test file/key so dedup behaviour is independently verifiable.
findings.finding: AC4 ('Dedup vs E6.no_hedges specified and tested') names no proving command, no test file, and no test key — leaving 'tested' as an effort-framed assertion with no verifiable evidence path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC4
findings.suggested_fix: AC4: 'Dedup vs E6.no_hedges specified (same-location suppression) and tested. Proof: tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py -k dedup_hedge_same_location passes and demonstrates that only one finding is emitted when both detectors fire on the same location.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add named failure-path and boundary tests for (a) the scope-exclusion abstain branch (e.g., a case that should NOT abstain but could be misclassified as external-fact) and (b) the hedge detector's false-positive/invalid-input boundary, alongside the existing recall tests.
findings.finding: The scope-exclusion sub-check's abstain branch and the hedge detector's failure/invalid-input paths are new user-facing behaviors with no named failure-path or boundary tests. The scope-exclusion AC requires 'an abstain-with-coverage AC for external-fact exclusions' but names no test for that branch. The hedge-detector AC says only 'the chosen path has a recall test' — no failure, timeout, or invalid-input scenario is specified for either new behavior.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — E4 scope-exclusion sub-check AC; Acceptance Criteria — hedge-detector mechanism AC
findings.suggested_fix: In tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py, add a parametrized case for the abstain branch that verifies codebase exclusions are NOT abstained on, and a case for the hedge detector that verifies a non-hedged plan does not produce a hedge finding.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define update ownership for `committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim` writes from WS2's three new writers (E4 assertion, E4 scope-exclusion, hedge detector): specify whether writes are append-idempotent (keyed by finding location+type), who holds the write lock or merge authority, and document the retirement/reset path between review runs.
findings.finding: The shared sub-answer accumulator `committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim` gains multiple writers from WS2 (E4 confident-assertion path, E4 scope-exclusion sub-check, and the hedge detector), but the plan specifies no update ownership, no concurrency/idempotency semantics for concurrent or retried writes, and no retirement/cleanup path within WS2.
findings.location: What section + AC (all ACs feed into the shared sub-answer); WS1 dependency note
findings.suggested_fix: Key each finding entry by (detector_id, location, finding_type) and use an upsert/merge rather than blind append; document that WS1 owns the retirement path (clearing the accumulator at run start) and that WS2 writers are idempotent given that key scheme.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rule out the exec:DET-into-verifier option explicitly (Engine B scans code-file repos, not prose; no existing prose-scan path exists) and commit to the 1-TURN finder pattern, which already has 17+ call-sites in criteria_routing.json.
findings.finding: The plan proposes exec:DET as the hedge-detector mechanism, forcing Engine B (an opengrep/ast-grep code-file scanner, `det_invariants.py:112 engine_b.scan(repo_root, ...)`) onto plan-prose strings — a golden-hammer misapplication. Engine B's entire scan surface (`_run_opengrep`, `_run_astgrep`) operates on repo directories of code files; there is no prose-string input path. The plan itself flags this as an OPEN DESIGN ISSUE but leaves the golden-hammer option open rather than ruling it out.
findings.location: What section — G-7(b) exec:DET hedge detector; also AC: 'Hedge-detector mechanism DECIDED and documented (exec:DET-into-verifier vs 1-TURN finder)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the design note (AC: 'Hedge-detector mechanism DECIDED'), record: 'exec:DET is ruled out — Engine B has no prose-string input path (det_invariants.py:112 passes a repo_root directory); the hedge detector is implemented as a 1-TURN finder criterion in criteria_routing.json, consistent with the existing single-turn hedge checks.' Remove the DET-into-verifier branch from the decision space.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proof step to AC 4: name the file where the same-location suppression rule is specified (e.g., plan_review_E4.md or the hedge detector module) and add a test key (e.g., tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py -k hedge_dedup_e6) mirroring AC 1's format.
findings.finding: AC 4 ('Dedup vs E6.no_hedges specified (same-location suppression) and tested') has no concrete verification step — unlike AC 1 (which specifies a grep command and a named test file), AC 4 provides no file path, grep command, or test reference to confirm 'specified' is verifiable.
findings.location: AC item 4
findings.suggested_fix: AC 4 revised proof: 'Proof: dedup rule documented in plan_review_E4.md (or hedge detector module); grep for E6.no_hedges suppression logic; add test in tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py -k hedge_dedup_e6.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before building the same-location suppression dedup mechanism, confirm the hedge detector's scope is demonstrably non-overlapping with E6.no_hedges (e.g., 'plan body/Why/What prose' vs 'AC text only'); if overlap is real, extend E6's checklist rather than add a parallel detector plus a new suppression abstraction (Rule-of-Three: 0 existing call-sites for cross-criterion same-location suppression).
findings.finding: The proposed hedge detector duplicates functionality already present in `no_hedges` (E6, `criteria_routing.json:224`), which checks 'No red-flag hedges ("should","probably","seems") standing in for a proving command.' The plan's proposed 'same-location suppression' dedup mechanism has zero existing call-sites in the codebase — it is a new abstraction invented for a single cross-criterion dedup use-case, violating Rule-of-Three before it is built.
findings.location: What section — G-7(b); AC: 'Dedup vs E6.no_hedges specified (same-location suppression) and tested'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fallback/degradation path to the hedge-detector design: specify what is delivered if neither exec:DET-into-verifier nor 1-TURN-finder can be fully routed within WS2 scope (e.g., a stub abstain-with-coverage result, or a deferred WS3 ticket).
findings.finding: The hedge-detector (G-7(b)) is explicitly an unresolved design fork with two paths ('exec:DET-into-verifier' vs '1-TURN finder'), and the plan specifies no fallback or degradation behaviour if neither path is viable. The plan tells the executor 'Resolve BEFORE building' but provides no safety net: if the resolution concludes that both options are blocked (e.g. DET cannot scan prose and a 1-TURN finder cannot be routed into the verifier), the hedge signal is simply absent with no documented handling.
findings.location: What section G-7(b); AC 3

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete abstain-branch AC (with a named proving command or test case) to the Acceptance Criteria list — e.g., 'given an external-fact exclusion claim, E4 emits an abstain-with-coverage record; verified by test_plan_review_evals.py -k scope_exclusion_abstain'.
findings.finding: The 'abstain-with-coverage AC for external-fact exclusions' is referenced in the What section as something to add, but no such AC appears in the Acceptance Criteria list — creating a gap between the stated What and the verifiable done-state.
findings.location: What section, G-4; Acceptance Criteria, AC2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen AC 3's proof step to explicitly require a routing-correctness test (e.g., assert findings appear in verifier output, not raw partition_findings) when exec:DET-into-verifier is chosen, OR state that 1-TURN finder is chosen and close the routing concern explicitly.
findings.finding: AC 3's verify step ('design note in ticket + the chosen path has a recall test') does not explicitly require verification that the DET-bypass-Pass-2 routing constraint is resolved — the What section declares this must be 'Resolved BEFORE building' but the AC only requires a decision document and a recall test, which could pass even if the routing problem is unaddressed.
findings.location: What section G-7(b) vs. AC item 3
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC 3: 'If exec:DET-into-verifier: add an integration test asserting hedge findings appear in verifier output (not bypassed into raw partition_findings). If 1-TURN finder: document that DET bypass is not applicable.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a time-boxed spike (≤1 day) to validate the chosen hedge-detector path before full build-out: for exec:DET-into-verifier, prove that det_invariants.py can accept a prose string and route its finding through the verifier; for 1-TURN finder, prove dedup with E6.no_hedges works end-to-end on a fixture plan.
findings.finding: The exec:DET prose-scanning path (or its 1-TURN alternative) is a novel, unproven architecture — DET currently scans code files, not plan-prose strings, and the rerouting into the verifier is unvalidated. The plan says 'Resolve BEFORE building' but provides no spike, prototype, or fixture to empirically establish that either path is feasible before full build-out.
findings.location: ## What (with review-surfaced corrections) — G-7(b) OPEN DESIGN ISSUE; AC: 'Hedge-detector mechanism DECIDED and documented'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'Spike result documented in ticket: [exec:DET path] OR [1-TURN finder path] demonstrated on a synthetic fixture plan, with at least one hedge correctly detected and correctly deduped vs E6.no_hedges, before story moves to In Progress.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the chosen hedge-detector architecture (exec:DET-into-verifier vs 1-TURN finder, and its Pass 2 routing contract) in a durable artifact — ADR, CLAUDE.md section, or named design doc — not only in the ticket.
findings.finding: The novel architectural decision (exec:DET-into-verifier vs 1-TURN finder, and the chosen Pass 2 routing) is not captured in any ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design doc update. This is a cross-pipeline-stage integration choice with long-term maintainability implications.
findings.location: ## What — G-7(b); AC: 'Hedge-detector mechanism DECIDED and documented'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify error handling and observability for each new failure point: (a) exec:DET invocation — surface tool-unavailable as a named error finding rather than silent empty result; (b) LLM call for E4/hedge — define retry/timeout behavior and emit a metric or log on failure.
findings.finding: The plan adds new failure points — at minimum one new LLM call path (E4 enhanced scan or 1-TURN hedge finder) and potentially an exec:DET invocation (opengrep/ast-grep on prose) — with no error handling, observability, or degraded-mode behavior specified for these additions.
findings.location: ## What — G-7(a) E4 scan, G-7(b) hedge detector; Acceptance Criteria

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or note) confirming that existing `test_plan_review_evals.py` E4 tests remain green after the lexicon addition, or explicitly document which existing tests need updating to reflect the new assertion-detection scope.
findings.finding: E4's behavior is being changed (new confident-assertion lexicon added, new scope-exclusion sub-check added), but the plan does not acknowledge whether existing E4 tests remain valid or require updating — only a new recall test is specified.
findings.location: AC: 'add recall case in tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py -k confident_assertion'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider splitting the hedge-detector mechanism (G-7(b)) into a child or sibling ticket so the settled E4 lexicon + scope-exclusion work can ship independently once the hedge design is decided; at minimum, document why the coupling is necessary if they must stay together.
findings.finding: The hedge-detector (G-7(b)) is an explicitly unresolved design concern ('OPEN DESIGN ISSUE — do NOT treat as settled') bundled into the same ticket as the E4 enhancements. The two outcome sets — (a) E4 lexicon + scope-exclusion sub-check and (b) hedge detector mechanism + dedup — are independently valuable and independently testable once the design decision is made. Bundling an undecided architectural choice (exec:DET-into-verifier vs 1-TURN finder) with settled E4 work creates scope uncertainty that could stall delivery of the E4 pieces.
findings.location: What section / G-7(b) hedge detector; Acceptance Criteria — hedge-detector AC

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve `plan_review_E4.md`: either identify the real file path (e.g. a prompt YAML or criteria_routing.json entry) where the E4 lexicon should live, or explicitly declare this as a new file creation and add it to `file_impact`.
findings.finding: `plan_review_E4.md` is named as the primary edit target throughout the plan (What section items G-7(a) and G-4, AC1, AC2) but does not exist anywhere in the repository — a full-repo search returns zero matches.
findings.location: What section (G-7(a), G-4); AC1; AC2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly classify each deliverable hunk (E4 lexicon, scope-exclusion sub-check, hedge detector) as in-scope modification of an existing file vs. new-file creation, and acknowledge any CREATION items as intentional scope additions.
findings.finding: All three deliverables (E4 lexicon in plan_review_E4.md, E4 scope-exclusion sub-check, exec:DET hedge detector) are CREATION of new behavior, but the plan classifies them implicitly as modifications to existing artifacts. The behavioral classification is ambiguous/missing from the plan.
findings.location: What section (G-7(a), G-4, G-7(b))

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before building G-7(b): evaluate Option A (prose-scanning DET extension) against Option B (1-TURN finder) with explicit reference to the fact that exec:DET has no prose-scanning capability today; document in the design note WHY the chosen path is preferred over the established 1-TURN alternative, or confirm Option B is selected.
findings.finding: Option A of the G-7(b) hedge-detector design ('add a prose-scanning DET path routed INTO the verifier') asserts that exec:DET can be adapted to scan plan-prose strings, but the DET tier (engine_b.py / det_invariants.py) is exclusively a code-file scanner (opengrep/ast-grep/scc/gitleaks over source files) with no existing prose-scanning capability. This unverified capability assertion is left open alongside the well-established alternative (Option B: 1-TURN finder), with no evaluation of Option A's architectural novelty vs. Option B's proven pattern.
findings.location: What section — G-7(b) exec:DET hedge detector, OPEN DESIGN ISSUE; AC 3 (hedge-detector mechanism DECIDED)
findings.suggested_fix: Select Option B (1-TURN finder) — it has direct prior art in E2/G5/E6 and avoids inventing a new DET prose-scanning backend. Record the decision in the design note: 'Option A rejected: DET (engine_b) scans code files only; a prose-scanning DET path would require novel infrastructure with no prior art; Option B (1-TURN finder) satisfies the requirement with zero new architecture.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
